raw_response:
I concur with the assertion made previously that option D represents an instance where optic atrophy does not generally manifest. My rationale rests upon recognizing that unlike several alternatives linked explicitly to compromised functionality along vital conduits responsible for conveying light stimuli processing details from eyes toward brain regions interpreting them visually—an aspect crucial during normal operation functioning—it remains relatively uninvolved throughout entire process making likelihood significantly lower chances encountering any form deterioration visible clinically known medically termed 'optic atrophy'.
